017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.health;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
020
021/**
022 * Shared, observable readiness flag consumed by {@link HealthAggregator} to gate the readiness probe.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * This is the building block for zero-downtime shutdown.  When a microservice begins shutting down it flips
026 * the readiness state {@link #markOutOfService() out of service} <i>before</i> the listening connector stops.
027 * The {@link HealthAggregator} consults this state for the {@link HealthProbe#READY READY} probe so
028 * {@code /readyz} returns {@code 503} (out of service) as soon as shutdown begins &mdash; this lets a load
029 * balancer / Kubernetes stop routing new traffic to the pod while in-flight requests drain.  The
030 * {@link HealthProbe#LIVE LIVE} probe ({@code /livez}) is intentionally <b>not</b> gated, so Kubernetes does
031 * not kill the pod mid-drain.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Resolution:</h5>
034 * <p>
035 * {@link HealthAggregator} and the embedded-server shutdown hooks resolve the state via
036 * {@link #resolve(BeanStore)}: a {@code ReadinessState} bean registered in the relevant {@link BeanStore} wins;
037 * otherwise the process-wide {@link #shared() shared} instance is used.  In the standalone microservice runtime
038 * the auto-mounted health probe servlet and the server lifecycle component live in <i>different</i> bean stores,
039 * so they bridge through the shared instance &mdash; the server flips it on stop and the probe observes the flip.
040 *
041 * <p>
042 * This class is thread-safe; the flag is {@code volatile}.
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='jc'>{@link HealthAggregator}
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * @since 10.0.0
049 */
050public final class ReadinessState {
051
052   private static final ReadinessState SHARED = new ReadinessState();
053
054   /**
055    * Returns the process-wide shared readiness state.
056    *
057    * <p>
058    * Used as the fallback when no {@code ReadinessState} bean is registered in the relevant {@link BeanStore}.
059    * In the standalone microservice runtime this is the bridge between the embedded-server lifecycle component
060    * (which flips it on shutdown) and the auto-mounted health probe servlet (which observes the flip), since the
061    * two live in separate bean stores.
062    *
063    * @return The shared instance.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
064    */
065   public static ReadinessState shared() {
066      return SHARED;
067   }
068
069   /**
070    * Resolves the readiness state from the given bean store, falling back to the {@link #shared() shared} instance.
071    *
072    * @param beanStore The bean store to consult.  Can be <jk>null</jk> (resolves to the shared instance).
073    * @return The resolved readiness state.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
074    */
075   public static ReadinessState resolve(BeanStore beanStore) {
076      if (beanStore == null)
077         return SHARED;
078      return beanStore.getBean(ReadinessState.class).orElse(SHARED);
079   }
080
081   private volatile boolean ready = true;
082
083   /**
084    * Constructor.
085    *
086    * <p>
087    * The new state starts {@link #isReady() ready}.
088    */
089   public ReadinessState() {
090      // Intentionally empty — the volatile `ready` field defaults to true.
091   }
092
093   /**
094    * Returns whether this service is currently ready to receive traffic.
095    *
096    * @return <jk>true</jk> if ready, <jk>false</jk> if out of service.
097    */
098   public boolean isReady() {
099      return ready;
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Marks this service as ready to receive traffic.
104    *
105    * <p>
106    * Called when an embedded server starts so a freshly-(re)started service is ready.
107    *
108    * @return This object.
109    */
110   public ReadinessState markReady() {
111      ready = true;
112      return this;
113   }
114
115   /**
116    * Marks this service as out of service so the readiness probe ({@code /readyz}) returns {@code 503}.
117    *
118    * <p>
119    * Called at the very beginning of shutdown, before the listening connector stops.
120    *
121    * @return This object.
122    */
123   public ReadinessState markOutOfService() {
124      ready = false;
125      return this;
126   }
127}
